items.summary: Memory is promotion-ready: Gitignoring a directory with `local-data/` blocks `!local-data/recall-probes.json` re-include rules — git refuses to...
items.reason: Recalled 2 times and backed by 2 sources, so it is a good candidate for canonical wiki documentation.

records.text: Gitignoring a directory with `local-data/` blocks `!local-data/recall-probes.json` re-include rules — git refuses to descend into a fully-ignored directory. The working pattern is `local-data/*` (ignore contents, not the directory) followed by `!local-data/recall-probes.json`. Verify with `git check-ignore -v <path>` after editing .gitignore. This is the same trap that bit any future `!local-data/X.json` exemptions.

actions.label: Apply promotion
actions.reason: Draft the promotion first to confirm the canonical target page before applying it.

items.summary: Memory is promotion-ready: Project-local memory IDs (`mem_xxx`) are per-machine — they are generated when `memory_remember` runs and never match...
items.reason: Recalled 2 times and backed by 2 sources, so it is a good candidate for canonical wiki documentation.

records.text: Project-local memory IDs (`mem_xxx`) are per-machine — they are generated when `memory_remember` runs and never match across operators. Recall probes that reference memories by ID alone are not portable across machines. For a probe set you want to commit and share, use the content-addressed matchers (`expectedTags`, `expectedRelatedFiles`, `expectedRelatedPages`) which match against memory content that's stable across operators. Each matcher within itself is logical AND across its array; a probe is satisfied by the first recalled memory matching ANY declared matcher with precedence id → tags → files → pages.
